capability: # templates/conformance.tsv — the CAPABILITY CONFORMANCE MATRIX (HERD-144): the committed proof map
# that binds each row of templates/capabilities.tsv to the proof(s) that exercise it, so coverage is
# accountable PER FEATURE. Read by `herd conformance` (bin/herd): `report` lists every capability with
# NO mapping (a gap) and every mapping whose proof_ref no longer exists (rot); `run` executes the mapped
# proofs and emits conformance.json. A SIBLING file, keyed by capability name, so templates/capabilities.tsv
# stays BYTE-IDENTICAL (its consumers — herd config/upgrade/codemap and the queued HERD-140/141/143 — are
# unaffected). TAB-separated; blank and '#'-prefixed lines ignored; the first line is the header.
# Columns:
#   capability   MUST match column 1 of templates/capabilities.tsv EXACTLY (the join key).
#   proof_kind   unit | sim | render — how the proof exercises the capability.
#                  unit   = a hermetic tests/*.sh (or *.bats) the healthcheck suite already runs.
#                  sim    = a scenario/simulation harness under tests/ (fidelity-ladder sims).
#                  render = a template/skill render assertion (the coordinator/hook render surface).
#   proof_ref    repo-root-relative path to the proof. `run --kind unit` executes it as `bash <ref>`.
# A capability may carry SEVERAL rows (several proofs); it is MAPPED when at least one proof_ref exists,
# GAP when it has no rows at all, and its individual missing-ref rows surface as ROT. UNMAPPED rows are
# NOT listed here — that is the point: the gap report derives them from capabilities.tsv MINUS this file,
# so they surface LOUDLY as none-yet rather than being silently omitted.
# SEED (HERD-144): the config-key family only, so the gap report starts HONEST — every other capability
# reads as an explicit gap until its own proof mapping lands. Grow this file as coverage is claimed.
herd config <list|get|set>
herd config <list|get|set>
herd config <list|get|set>
herd config <list|get|set>
herd config <list|get|set>
